fixtures.description: One-dimensional rational linear flow with a threshold-triggered commitment update.
Two-dimensional computable globally Lipschitz nonlinear flow with certified interval refinement.
Piecewise-rational control and isolated guard crossing with a declared reset and revision history.
A fixed finite time grid is asserted to be the exact continuous trajectory and misses an intervening guard crossing.
The representation invokes undeclared exact-real equality and future-trajectory oracles.
A non-Lipschitz initial-value problem with multiple solutions and no frozen selection rule.
A hybrid system with infinitely many events in finite time.
